data.title: US media: Iranian parliament speaker threatens to cut off shipping through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait
data.description: According to a report by NBC News on April 3, Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf appeared to threaten to block commercial shipping through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a vital chokepoint in the Red Sea. Ghalibaf reportedly posted on social media platform X, asking, "What percentage of global shipments of oil, liquefied natural gas, wheat, rice, and fertilizer pass through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait?" He continued, "Which countries and companies account for the largest share of cargo transported through this strait?" Ships transiting the area have been repeatedly attacked in recent years by the Houthi rebels in Yemen, allied with the Iranian regime. This threat to the Bab el-Mandeb Strait comes at a time when Iran has already largely cut off shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has thrown the global economy into chaos. (Reference News)

data.title: Iranian Speaker Threatens Red Sea Shipping Route
data.description:
Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has reportedly threatened to disrupt commercial shipping traffic through the Mandeb Strait, a crucial Red Sea passage. According to BlockBeats, this statement was made following a report by NBC News on April 3.

Ghalibaf questioned on social media platform X about the global share of oil, liquefied natural gas, wheat, rice, and fertilizer transported through the Mandeb Strait. He further inquired about which countries and companies have the highest shipping volumes through this route.

In recent years, vessels passing through this area have repeatedly faced attacks from the Houthi armed group in Yemen, which is allied with the Iranian regime. The threat to the Mandeb Strait comes as Iran has already significantly disrupted shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, causing turmoil in the global economy.

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description: 1. British media: Britain seeks to attract Anthropic to expand its business in the UK; 2. Iranian media: A US military plane searching for its pilots was shot down in Isfahan province; 3. Trump: US military completes "most daring search and rescue operation" in history, all missing pilots have been rescued; 4. The probability of "US military presence in Iran as of April 30" on Polymarket briefly rose to 96.5%, an increase of 11% in 24 hours; 5. International Energy Agency Executive Director: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed, and losses in crude oil and refined products will double in April; 6. Drift: The April 1 attack was a long-term infiltration operation planned by a North Korean hacking group; 7. University of Connecticut reaches the 3rd Madness finals for the 7th time: Polymarket's probability of winning rises to 32%, an increase of 18% in 24 hours; 8. Western Union completes acquisition of Singapore digital wallet Dash.

data.title: British media: Britain seeks to attract Anthropic to expand its operations in the UK.
data.description: According to the Financial Times, the UK is trying to persuade Anthropic to expand its operations in the country. The report, citing sources familiar with the matter, states that the UK government's proposals to Anthropic range from expanding its London office to a dual listing. Neither Anthropic nor the UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology immediately responded to Reuters' requests for comment. The office of Prime Minister Keir Starmer has been supporting the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology's efforts, and the proposals will be presented to Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei during his visit to the UK at the end of May. The US government previously blacklisted Anthropic, deeming it a national security supply chain risk because Anthropic refused to allow the military to use its AI chatbot, Claude, in US surveillance or autonomous weapons systems. A US judge has temporarily blocked the blacklist, and the AI ​​startup has filed a second lawsuit over this supply chain risk assessment, which is still pending. (Jinshi)

data.title: AI TRENDS | UK Seeks Anthropic Expansion Amid U.S. Blacklist Concerns
data.description:
The UK is reportedly attempting to persuade AI company Anthropic to expand its operations within the country, according to the Financial Times. The British government has proposed options ranging from expanding Anthropic's office in London to pursuing a dual listing. According to Jin10, neither Anthropic nor the UK's Department for Science, Innovation and Technology have responded to Reuters' requests for comment. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's office is supporting the department's efforts, with plans to present the proposal to Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei during his visit to the UK at the end of May.

Anthropic has been blacklisted by the U.S. government, which cited national security supply chain risks due to the company's refusal to allow its AI chatbot, Claude, to be used for U.S. surveillance or autonomous weapon systems. A U.S. judge has temporarily halted the implementation of this blacklist, and Anthropic has filed a second lawsuit challenging the supply chain risk designation, which is currently under review.

data.title: Bitcoin Sentiment Turns Bearish as Fear Index Remains Low
data.description: Social media sentiment towards Bitcoin has become increasingly bearish, reaching its highest level in five weeks. According to NS3.AI, Santiment's bullish-to-bearish comment ratio dropped to 0.81 on Saturday. Additionally, the Crypto Fear & Greed Index remained in the Extreme Fear category, holding steady at 12 on Sunday.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts High Probability of Major Crypto Hack by Year-End
data.description: The probability of another cryptocurrency hack resulting in losses exceeding $100 million by December 31 is currently estimated at 57% on Polymarket. According to Foresight News, this prediction includes potential attacks on decentralized exchanges and lending protocols.

data.title: Iranian media: A US military plane searching for its pilot was shot down in Isfahan province.
data.description: Iran's Tasnim News Agency: The Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claims that a U.S. military aircraft searching for its pilot was shot down in Isfahan province. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trump: U.S. military completes "most daring search and rescue operation" in history; all missing pilots rescued.
data.description: US President Trump posted on social media: "My fellow Americans, in the past few hours the United States Military has conducted one of the most daring search and rescue operations in American history, targeting one of our distinguished officer crew members. I am thrilled to announce that he is now safe and sound! This brave soldier was behind enemy lines, trapped in the treacherous mountains of Iran, being hunted by our enemies who were closing in every hour. But he was never truly alone, as his Commander-in-Chief, Secretary of War, Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, and his comrades monitored his location around the clock and meticulously planned his rescue. Under my direction, the United States Military deployed dozens of aircraft, carrying the world's most lethal weapons, to rescue the missing pilot. He was injured but will make a full recovery. This miraculous search and rescue operation followed the successful rescue of another brave pilot yesterday. We did not confirm that operation earlier because we did not want to jeopardize the second rescue. We will never abandon an American soldier!" The fact that we were able to complete these two operations without a single American killed or wounded once again proves that we have achieved overwhelming air superiority over Iran.

data.title: U.S. Military Executes Daring Rescue Operation
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced on April 5 that the U.S. military has completed one of the most daring rescue operations in American history. According to BlockBeats, the operation involved rescuing a remarkable crew member. Under Trump's directive, the military deployed dozens of aircraft equipped with the world's 'most lethal weapons' to recover the missing pilot. The pilot sustained injuries but is expected to make a full recovery.

data.title: Bitcoin Developers Explore Quantum Defense Amid Google Research Findings
data.description: Bitcoin developers are evaluating proposals for quantum defense following Google's recent research indicating that a sufficiently powerful quantum computer could potentially break Bitcoin's core cryptography in less than nine minutes. According to NS3.AI, the article discusses several initiatives, including BIP 360, post-quantum signature schemes, a commit/reveal design for mempool protection, and Hourglass V2. However, it is noted that none of these proposals have been activated yet.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts High Probability of U.S. Military Entering Iran by April 30
data.description:
The probability of U.S. military forces entering Iran by April 30 has surged to 96.5% on Polymarket, marking an 11% increase within 24 hours. According to Odaily, this spike reflects heightened speculation in the prediction market.

An American government official informed Al Jazeera that the second crew member from a downed F-15E fighter jet has been rescued, although the rescue operation is still ongoing. The rescued crew member is confirmed safe but not yet out of danger, as the rescue team must successfully evacuate from Iran and return safely.

Polymarket's rules state that the market will be deemed "yes" if active U.S. military personnel enter Iranian land territory by the specified date (Eastern Time). Special operations forces are included, but intelligence personnel are not. Entry into Iranian waters or airspace does not count.

data.title: The probability of "US troops entering Iran as of April 30" on Polymarket has increased significantly, currently standing at 96.5%.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows a significant increase in the probability of "US military presence in Iran as of April 30" on Polymarket, currently standing at 96.5%. According to Al Jazeera, a US government official told Al Jazeera that the second crew member of the downed F-15E fighter jet has been rescued, and the rescue operation is ongoing. The missing crew member has been confirmed rescued but is not yet out of danger. They added that the rescue team still needs to successfully evacuate from Iran and return safely. According to Polymarket's criteria, if active-duty US military personnel actually entered Iran at any time before the specified date (Eastern Time), the market will rate it "Yes." Otherwise, the market will rate it "No." Special operations forces personnel qualify; however, intelligence personnel are not included. US military personnel must actually enter Iranian land territory to qualify. Entry into Iranian waters or airspace is not counted. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: The second crew member of the downed F-15E fighter jet has been rescued.
data.description: According to Al Jazeera: A U.S. government official said the second crew member of the downed F-15E fighter jet has been rescued after a “fierce firefight.” (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Government Confirms Rescue of Second Crew Member from Downed F-15E Fighter Jet
data.description: A U.S. government official announced the successful rescue of the second crew member from a downed F-15E fighter jet following intense combat. According to BlockBeats, the incident occurred on April 5, as reported by Al Jazeera. The rescue operation was carried out after the aircraft was shot down during a period of heavy fighting. Further details about the circumstances surrounding the incident and the condition of the rescued crew member have not been disclosed.

data.title: U.S. and Iran Engage in Indirect Talks Amid Tensions
data.description:
In recent developments, the United States and Iran have been involved in indirect negotiations through Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey over the past ten days, aiming to reach a ceasefire agreement contingent on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. According to BlockBeats, these discussions have yet to yield any substantial progress.

The indirect talks are led by U.S. Vice President Vance and Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf, with Pakistan's military chief, Marshal Asim Munir, mediating. White House envoy Whitkov, along with foreign ministers from Iran, Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt, are also participating in the negotiations. Two sources involved in the talks have disclosed that mediators are still striving to facilitate a direct meeting between the U.S. and Iran, but efforts have been largely unproductive so far.

One source indicated that Iran has rejected any proposals for a temporary ceasefire, demanding a permanent end to hostilities and clear assurances from the U.S. that it will not launch further attacks. Mediators are currently exploring potential trust-building measures as a pathway to direct U.S.-Iran negotiations, though it remains uncertain whether any progress will be made before the Monday deadline.

data.title: IEA Chief Warns of Oil Supply Risks Amid Hormuz Strait Closure
data.description:
The International Energy Agency (IEA) Chief, Fatih Birol, has issued a warning regarding the potential doubling of oil and refined product losses in April if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed to shipping. According to Odaily, Birol emphasized that even if the conflict ends, it will take a significant amount of time to return to normalcy.

Birol stated that the IEA is closely monitoring all critical energy assets in the region, including oil and gas fields, pipelines, refineries, and liquefied natural gas terminals. He noted that 72 energy assets have been damaged, with one-third suffering severe or very severe damage.

He commended Saudi Arabia for its swift response to the crisis, highlighting the country's rerouting of over two-thirds of its oil exports through a pipeline to the Red Sea. Birol mentioned that Saudi authorities assured him of the pipeline's robust protection. However, he cautioned that any attack on this route could have extremely serious consequences for the global economy.

data.title: International Energy Agency Executive Director: Continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz will double losses on crude oil and refined products in April.
data.description: International Energy Agency (IEA) Executive Director Fatih Birol warned that if the Strait of Hormuz does not reopen to shipping, the amount of crude oil and refined products lost in April will be double the amount lost in March. Even after the conflict ends, it will take a long time to return to normal. “We are monitoring all critical energy assets in the region hourly,” he said, referring to oil and gas fields, pipelines, refineries, and liquefied natural gas terminals. “Currently, 72 energy assets have been damaged, and one-third of them are severely or very severely damaged,” he added. Birol praised Saudi Arabia’s swift response to the crisis, noting that the country diverted more than two-thirds of its oil exports via a pipeline to the Red Sea. Birol said that Saudi Arabia’s highest authorities assured him that the critical pipeline was well protected. However, Birol pointed out that if this route were attacked, the consequences for the global economy would be extremely severe. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Spot ETF Sees Inflows While Ethereum Spot ETF Experiences Outflows
data.description: This week, U.S. Bitcoin spot ETFs recorded a net inflow of $22.2 million, according to BlockBeats. In contrast, Ethereum spot ETFs experienced a net outflow of $42.1 million, as reported by Farside monitoring data.

data.title: UConn Advances to March Madness Finals with Increased Championship Odds
data.description: UConn secured a spot in the March Madness finals by defeating Illinois 71-62. According to NS3.AI, Polymarket has adjusted UConn's championship probability to 32%, marking an 18% increase over the past 24 hours. In another game, Michigan led Arizona with a score of 48-32. Polymarket's data indicates Michigan's probability at 62%, while Arizona stands at 6%.
